channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Montreal Police Complete Second Day of Search for Missing Man in St. Lawrence River
item.description: Montreal police have concluded the second day of a search for a missing man in the St. Lawrence River, following a multi-agency effort to locate the individual. The search, which began after the man w...

item.title: China and Namibia Strengthen Bilateral Ties Through Strategic Cooperation
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Namibian President Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah held high-level talks in Beijing, agreeing to elevate their bilateral relationship by fostering a shared future for developme...

item.title: How to Pay Kenya Power Bills via M-Pesa and Avoid Common Errors
item.description: Kenya Power customers can now pay their electricity bills through M-Pesa by following specific steps. Prepaid users receive a token code to load onto their meters, while postpaid customers must clear ...

item.title: University professor fears masked intruders during antisemitism inquiry
item.description: A University of Melbourne physics professor has recounted his experience of discovering approximately 20 masked intruders in his campus office, describing the moment of uncertainty about whether the i...

item.title: UN Experts Condemn India's Voter Deletion Practices Targeting Muslims
item.description: Three United Nations special rapporteurs have raised serious concerns about India's mass voter deletion process, alleging that millions of voters—particularly Muslims—were wrongfully removed from elec...

item.title: Edmonton deals with sinkhole and flooding aftermath after severe storms
item.description: A massive sinkhole measuring 12x12 feet and over 10 meters deep has formed near the intersection of 130 Avenue and 107 Street in Edmonton's Lauderdale neighborhood following intense weekend thundersto...

item.title: Oriire school abduction: Children beaten, teachers chained as rescue operation succeeds
item.description: A group of 39 pupils and seven teachers were abducted in Oriire Local Government Area, Oyo State, and held captive for 56 days. The principal, Mrs Racheal Alamu, revealed that children were beaten int...

item.title: Supreme Court Addresses POCSO Misuse and Elopement of Teenagers
item.description: The Supreme Court recently raised concerns about the misuse of POCSO (Protection of Children from Sexual Offences) provisions against teenagers in voluntary sexual relationships. A bench of Justices B...

item.title: National Treasury Intervenes to Address Local Government Financial Mismanagement
item.description: The National Treasury has taken decisive action to address the severe financial mismanagement plaguing South Africa's local municipalities. By temporarily withholding equitable share transfers for Jul...

item.title: Fed Governor Comments on Inflation Outlook and Policy Path
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller addressed the U.S. economy's inflation trajectory during a recent speech, emphasizing that persistent price pressures remain a key concern for policymakers....

item.title: Cameron Munster's knee injury threatens Melbourne Storm's NRL finals hopes
item.description: Melbourne Storm have suffered a significant setback as star five-eighth Cameron Munster will undergo knee surgery, potentially ruling him out for the rest of the regular season. The injury occurs just...

item.title: Shakespeare’s Love’s Labour’s Lost Reimagined with Flamenco at the Globe Theatre
item.description: Director Indiana Lown-Collins, half-Spanish and raised in Spain, is merging flamenco with Shakespeare’s early comedy *Love’s Labour’s Lost* at the Globe Theatre. The production explores themes of pass...

item.title: Montreal Residents Frustrated by Roadwork Disruptions and Lack of Notice
item.description: A Montreal woman, Alison Aylward, has expressed frustration after returning home from a 10-day vacation to find her property effectively cut off by ongoing roadwork in the Notre-Dame-de-Grâce neighbou...

item.title: Tanzania's Vision 2050 Funding Challenges and Strategies
item.description: Tanzania's Development Vision 2050 requires significant financial resources to achieve its goals in infrastructure, education, healthcare, agriculture, and industrial development. The country faces ch...

item.title: UK proposes legal changes to deport convicted paedophile Shabir Ahmed
item.description: The UK Home Minister, Shabana Mahmood, announced plans to amend immigration laws to allow the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, a convicted paedophile and former ringleader of the Rochdale grooming gang. A...

item.title: Class 11 student dies after accidental fall from 10th floor in Bhubaneswar
item.description: A Class 11 student allegedly died after falling from the 10th floor of a residential apartment in Bhubaneswar, Odisha. According to police, the boy went to meet his female classmate when she was alone...

item.title: Government mandates battery storage in power auctions to tackle grid challenges
item.description: The Pakistani government has mandated battery energy storage systems (BESS) as a mandatory requirement for upcoming power auctions to address grid challenges like the duck curve. This decision comes a...

item.title: Nigeria's import duty waivers reached ₦34 trillion as Customs highlights security and economic support measures
item.description: The Comptroller-General of the Nigeria Customs Service (NCS), Bashir Adeniyi, told the Senate Committee on Finance that import duty waivers granted through Import Duty Exemption Certificates (IDECs) r...

item.title: Adeyemi Faces Charges Over PFIPC Scandal as US Firm Offers Asylum Support
item.description: The embattled Director-General of the controversial Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeyemi Adeniyi, has declared readiness to face charges related to the scandal involvin...

item.title: Health Canada report highlights regulatory failures at Grifols plasma donation clinics
item.description: A Health Canada inspection report has revealed significant regulatory lapses at the private plasma collection company Grifols, including repeated clearance of ineligible donors at risk for Creutzfeldt...
